principle: Automatic prompts are injected only when they materially change routing, safety, quality, or verification. Availability alone is never a trigger.

when: observable outcome, audience, scope, authority, evidence lane, medium, or acceptance criteria is route-changing and unresolved
target prompt has a preferred skill whose value is unresolved
native execution may miss a material acceptance criterion
MD-192 classifies the capability as required or materially beneficial
no installed conformant skill satisfies it
automatic acquisition policy or explicit approval permits installation
exact installed skill is task-fit and available
skill use materially improves an acceptance criterion
finite queue has multiple items or measurable iterative refinement is required
each pass can produce new evidence or state
reject_when: cost or side effects exceed expected gain

genericity_rule: Loop and skill-execution prompts accept any prompt, scenario, or exact skill. Domain examples never narrow eligibility.
questioning_rule: All routes may invoke MD-191, but only when a question can change routing, authority, evidence, medium, budget, skill fit, loop eligibility, or acceptance.
